title: option<T>
manager: `option<T>` — a value that is either `some(x)` or `none` — the type that replaces null-checks with something the compiler forces you to unwrap. It is what `get` returns on a container miss, so "the key might not be there" becomes a fact in the type instead of a null-pointer waiting to happen. Pound for pound the cheapest defect-prevention type in the language.
cs: option<T> = some(x) | none; the return of get/find (W6.43); unwrap via case (W6.41); interacts with T? (W6.25).
